items.title: Pick a hero image — Day14 core landing
items.summary: Three brand-hero candidates generated by cc-nano-banana for Day14, one per mood (calm authority / data-rich / human-warm). GEMINI_API_KEY missing — all three are placeholder cards at the cached path; re-run after setting the key to populate real PNGs at the same paths. Sign-off required — nothing was auto-applied to the live brand site.
items.voice_notes: Editorial, confident, anti-SaaS, anti-agency. Sharp, line-driven design. Single ember-orange accent (#FF5C28) against paper (#F8F6F1) and ink (#0B0B0A). Hairline rules, near-square edges, premium builder-y feel.

candidates.rationale: Confident, premium, restraint. The hero leads on craft + ownership, not motion. One bold ember accent against paper.
candidates.prompt: Editorial brand hero image for Day14 — a productized build studio that ships full branded business platforms (marketing site, customer portal, billing, admin app, AI chatbot) in 14 days. MOOD: calm authority. A single oversized industrial schematic drawing of a stack of layered platforms rendered in fine hairline ink linework on warm paper (#F8F6F1) — architectural blueprint feeling without any actual text labels. The composition is asymmetric and confident, with generous negative space, near-square edges, no drop shadows. A single restrained ember-orange (#FF5C28) accent appears as a thin keyline or punctuating shape — used with restraint, not as a fill. No words, no logos, no UI mockups. Photographic-quality editorial render, premium, builder-y, anti-SaaS.

The composition is asymmetric and confident, with generous negative space, near-square edges, no drop shadows. A single restrained ember-orange (#FF5C28) accent appears as a thin keyline or punctuating shape — used with restraint, not as a fill. No words, no logos, no UI mockups. Photographic-quality editorial render, premium, builder-y, anti-SaaS.
candidates.enrichment_reason: marketing-skills plugin not found on disk
candidates.enriched_prompt_text:
Editorial brand hero image for Day14 — a productized build studio that ships full branded business platforms (marketing site, customer portal, billing, admin app, AI chatbot) in 14 days. MOOD: calm authority. A single oversized industrial schematic drawing of a stack of layered platforms rendered in fine hairline ink linework on warm paper (#F8F6F1) — architectural blueprint feeling without any actual text labels. The composition is asymmetric and confident, with generous negative space, near-square edges, no drop shadows. A single restrained ember-orange (#FF5C28) accent appears as a thin keyline or punctuating shape — used with restraint, not as a fill. No words, no logos, no UI mockups. Photographic-quality editorial render, premium, builder-y, anti-SaaS.

--- Composition refinements (O8 enrichment) ---
Palette anchor: paper #F8F6F1, ink #0B0B0A, single accent #FF5C28 — ember-orange — single restrained accent, used as keyline or punctuation, never as fill.

Competitor-positioning constraints (what this image must NOT be):
  - anti-SaaS template
  - anti-agency theatre
  - anti-no-code generic
  - leads on ownership, hairlines, and craft — not motion or gradients

Compositional rules the render must honor:
  - negative-space ratio ≥ 0.5 of frame
  - near-square edges, no drop shadows, no soft glows
  - hairline rules at 0.5–0.75pt feel
  - single focal point at golden-ratio anchor (~0.382 from edge), not centered

Render contract: photographic editorial quality, 1536x1024, sharp focus, no synthetic blur or HDR, no over-saturation. Treat every additional constraint above as binding — do not relax them for visual interest.

candidates.rationale: Foregrounds the productized stack (3 SKUs, fixed price, 14-day timeline). Reads as evidence, not vibes.
candidates.prompt: Editorial brand hero image for Day14. MOOD: data-rich. A precise top-down arrangement of small architectural diagrams — exploded views of components like a marketing site, a customer portal, a billing flow, an admin app, an AI chatbot — drawn in fine ink linework on warm paper (#F8F6F1) and arranged on an underlying 8-pt grid that is faintly visible. Components float on the grid with hairline connecting rules between them; the negative space dominates. One element — the smallest one — glows in restrained ember-orange (#FF5C28). No legible text, no logos, no real product UI. Premium editorial illustration, 1536x1024, anti-SaaS, anti-agency, builder-y. Inspiration: technical drawings, naval architecture plates, Eames furniture diagrams.

candidates.rationale: Same palette + voice, but reads as a person at work — softens the brand without losing the editorial spine.
candidates.prompt: Editorial brand hero image for Day14. MOOD: human-warm. A wide overhead photograph of a single operator's workspace at golden hour: warm paper-colored desk (#F8F6F1), an open mechanical notebook with hand-drawn architecture sketches in ink, a small unbranded laptop showing only abstract ink-on-paper interface lines, a ceramic mug, a fountain pen — all arranged with generous negative space. The light is low and warm but the composition is still architectural and disciplined. One small object on the desk — a paper clip or a tag — is bright ember-orange (#FF5C28), the only saturated color in the frame. No visible text, no logos, no real product. 1536x1024. Premium magazine-feature photography style, confident, restrained.

items.prompt: OG/social card image for Day14 — Work with us page. Day14 is a productized build studio that ships full branded business platforms (marketing site, customer portal, billing, admin app, AI chatbot) in 14 days. The image is a landscape composition (1200x630, 16:9 aspect-ish) featuring a confident, premium editorial illustration on warm paper background (#F8F6F1) with hairline ink linework forming an architectural schematic of layered platforms. One restrained ember-orange (#FF5C28) accent. No text, no logos, no UI. Anti-SaaS, anti-agency, builder-y, builder-confident. Photographic-quality editorial render.

items.summary: Should the claude-mem bridge be allowed to write observations from Day14 daemons, or stay read-only?

payload.question: Should the claude-mem bridge be allowed to write? Default in the stub is read-only (CLAUDE_MEM_BRIDGE_WRITES unset → remember() returns {ok:false, reason:'writes-disabled'}). If writes are enabled, Day14 daemons can seed observations the autonomous pipeline already knows about (e.g. 'stop-slop ran on drafts/foo.mdx and removed 7 phrases'), making integration points (b) skill-history queries and (c) /admin agent continuity first-class — but daemon-written observations will be schema-drifted from the SDK-extracted ones, and there is no upstream-blessed label to mark them as non-Claude-Code.
options: Stay read-only — bridge never writes; recall() only. Safest, no schema drift.
Allow writes behind env flag CLAUDE_MEM_BRIDGE_WRITES=1 — opt-in per-daemon, off by default.
Allow writes unconditionally — daemons can seed observations freely; accept schema drift.
payload.context: Bridge stub at scripts/lib/skills/claude-mem.mjs currently returns {ok:false, reason:'not-wired'} for remember(). Upstream POST /api/sessions/observations accepts a synthesized claudeSessionId like 'day14-daemon-<uuid>'. Affects whether integration points (b) and (c) from §3 can be wired without manual mirroring.

items.summary: Should the bridge enforce a canonical project name per caller, or let claude-mem keep deriving project from cwd?

payload.question: Whose memory is this? The worker is single-user / single-database; the only tenant-scoping knob is the `project` field, which is cwd-derived. Are we OK letting `project` continue to be derived from working directory, or do we want the bridge to enforce a canonical project name per caller (e.g. 'studio', 'alignmd', 'life-loophole')? Enforcing it means tenant logic lives in the bridge, which Jack has historically pushed back on.
options: Keep cwd-derived project — no bridge-side tenant logic; trust each daemon's cwd.
Enforce canonical project per caller — bridge requires opts.project on every recall/remember call.
Hybrid — default to cwd, but allow opts.project override; warn (not error) on missing project.
payload.context: Cross-project searches (default scope) will surface observations from hot-flash-co/ and kennum-lawn-care/, both on the standing exclusion list. Privacy §5(iii) flagged this. Any bridge query should pass project:'studio' explicitly, but enforcement is the open question.

items.summary: When claude-mem isn't installed or the worker is down, should recall() hard-degrade ({ok:false}) or soft-degrade ({ok:true, items:[]})?

payload.question: What's the failure mode when claude-mem isn't installed? The bridge stub returns {ok:false, reason:'not-wired'} for everything today. When we wire it, two reasonable options: (i) hard-degrade — every recall() returns {ok:false} and callers must fall back to grep/WORK-LOG; (ii) soft-degrade — recall() returns an empty items:[] with ok:true and a source:'empty' marker, so callers don't have to branch on ok. Option (i) is more honest; option (ii) gets the integration adopted faster.
options: Hard-degrade — {ok:false, reason:'worker-unreachable'}; callers must branch and fall back to grep/WORK-LOG.
Soft-degrade — {ok:true, items:[], source:'empty'}; callers union the empty result without branching.
Hard-degrade for writes, soft-degrade for reads — remember() returns {ok:false}, recall() returns empty.
payload.context: Affects every caller's branch-handling. Hard-degrade keeps the contract honest but forces every call site to handle {ok:false}. Soft-degrade lets callers union claude-mem hits with existing grep results without conditionals, which matches the §3(a) 'complement, not replace' design.
